arts.ttl: Barcelona challenge LFP and RFEF over congested fixture scheduling
arts.sum: Barcelona criticise fixture timings, supporting coach's call for change. They plan a formal complaint to protect players, citing unfair scheduling in key competitions.

arts.ttl: Everton v Manchester City, Brentford v Brighton, Barcelona v Celta Vigo – live
arts.sum: Join Rob Smyth for updates from Saturday action around Europe with our clockwatch

18 April 2000 was one of the great Barça nights, when the club welcomed Chelsea to Camp Nou for the second leg of a Champions League quarter final, having lost 3-1 at Stamford Bridge and in need of something special to turn the tie around. Hansi Flick: "If they told me at the beginning that we'd be like this by April 19th, I'd accept it" - Mundo Deportivo

arts.ttl: 'It's a joke' - Hansi Flick on the war path in ongoing Barcelona feud
arts.sum: Hansi Flick was particularly angry when addressing the media about Barcelona's unrelenting fixture schedule.

arts.ttl: Barcelona vs Celta Vigo – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Barcelona can extend their advantage in the La Liga title race with a win against Celta Vigo on Saturday. The Catalans will move seven points clear of Real Madrid with a victory, ahead of the latter’s clash with Athletic Bilbao the following day. Sources close to the situation have informed CaughtOffside that Tonali has indicated that he could be open to leaving Newcastle United this summer. arts.ttl: Chelsea get deja vu as imposing Barcelona await in semi-final trilogy
arts.sum: Sonia Bompastor has her shot at ending the European champions’ reign and moving Blues step closer to history As Chelsea’s quest for a quadruple enters its final month, there is no more imposing obstacle to navigate than this. Barcelona, the European champions in three of the past four campaigns, are once again standing in the way of the English champions as Chelsea strive to lift the only major trophy that has eluded them, and there could be no more worthy adversary. There is more than a hint of deja vu about this semi-final. Not only because the first and second legs are taking place on the same dates, 20 April and 27 April, as they did last season, but also because it is the third straight year in which they have gone head-to-head at this stage, which both clubs can feel a touch unfortunate about. Once was tantalising. The rematch was welcome. The trilogy sounds enthralling but comes with a lingering sense that it might have been the final. Continue reading...
